sources.llm_title: Berkeley Master of Design - Berkeley MDes
sources.llm_summary: Describes Berkeley’s studio-based professional Master’s program and its audience, and provides the program name.
States that the Berkeley Master of Design is a professional graduate program in design for emerging technologies, jointly offered by the College of Engineering and College of Environmental Design.
Identifies the Berkeley Master of Design as a three-semester professional graduate degree.

derived_features.positioning_summary: A studio-based professional master’s in design for emerging technologies, jointly offered by engineering and design units, with explicit emphasis on critical design, social practice, and public-interest work. The program also highlights direct exposure to the Bay Area/Silicon Valley innovation ecosystem and is aimed at early- to mid-career professionals.

sources.llm_summary: States the program is a studio-based professional master’s, for emerging technologies, aimed at early- to mid-career professionals, and mentions exposure to the Bay Area design/technology sector and Silicon Valley innovation culture.

Describes the Berkeley MDes as a professional graduate program in design for emerging technologies, jointly offered by the College of Engineering and College of Environmental Design, and says it emphasizes a critical approach to design and social practice to address public-interest needs.

Summarizes the MDes as a professional graduate degree that prepares students for creative and technical roles and notes an interdisciplinary curriculum connecting technical rigor, design theory, and social practice.

derivation_notes.note: Used the program overview’s explicit language about a professional graduate program in design for emerging technologies, jointly offered by engineering and design-related units, with critical design and social practice/public-interest framing.
Used the admissions page’s explicit description of a studio-based professional master’s for early- to mid-career professionals and its mention of Bay Area/Silicon Valley innovation exposure.
professional: directly supported by 'studio-based professional Master’s program' and 'early to mid-career professionals'.

interdisciplinary: supported by the program being jointly offered by the College of Engineering and College of Environmental Design, and by the Graduate Division page’s 'interdisciplinary curriculum' wording.
stem_adjacent: supported by 'design for emerging technologies' and joint offering with the College of Engineering.
social_impact: supported by explicit references to 'social practice' and 'public interest' in program-authored copy.
Did not add digital or ui_ux because the evidence mentions emerging technologies and innovation exposure, but does not explicitly frame the program around digital products, interfaces, or UX.
Did not add research because the cited copy emphasizes professional practice, critical design, and social practice rather than scholarly research training.

sources.llm_summary: States that over three semesters (fall, spring, and a second fall) students complete the required coursework for the MDes degree.
Describes the Berkeley Master of Design (MDes) as a three-semester, professional graduate degree.

derivation_notes.note: Used the explicit 'over three semesters (fall, spring, and a second fall)' wording to set the program length to 3 Berkeley semesters.
Mapped the explicit three-semester duration to the allowed duration category 'three_semesters'.

sources.llm_summary: States the MDes requires a minimum of 39 units, charges per semester based on units enrolled, and presents an estimated total degree program cost table based on the minimum units for the degree.

States that MDes tuition and fees are charged by cohort on a per-unit basis and that the degree tuition and fees include the total tuition and campus fees for the minimum number of units over the three semesters of the program.

derivation_notes.note: high — official program fee table gives an explicit estimated total for the degree based on the minimum 39 units; computed as $2,109 × 39 = $82,251.
high — base currency is USD, so the comparison cost in USD matches the official estimated total degree cost.
Used the program’s own per-unit fee table and minimum-unit requirement rather than an external conversion or third-party estimate.
Set to tuition_only because the cited program pages describe tuition and campus fees for the degree, but not a full institution-published cost of attendance for all living expenses.
USD is implied by the official Berkeley fee table and dollar-denominated program fee schedule.
Set to 1 because the published fee amounts are already in USD; no currency conversion was needed.
Used the retrieval date as the reference date for the published fee table because no separate exchange-rate date was applicable.

derived_features.curriculum_summary: The MDes is a three-semester, 39-unit program with a required core sequence, a thesis studio, a capstone portfolio, and elective slots. The curriculum is studio-based but includes some flexibility through electives and a summer off-campus experience.

core_courses.course_summary: Introduces design frameworks and methods; the course critically examines the mindset, skillset, and toolset associated with design, interweaving readings and presentations on design methods, strategy, and the history of design and technology.

Builds foundational design and technology frameworks for designing products, services, and experiences enabled by emerging technologies, using a human-centered design process that includes research and related design methods.

Introduces MDes students to the key concepts of design studio and the foundational principles and methods that shape how designers work, collaborate, and practice; includes the culture and life of a working studio.
Examines debates, critical perspectives, and contemporary issues in design, helping students engage with contemporary design discourse and positioning.
Advanced course focused on designing with emerging technologies in a project-based format.

Culminating course for the MDes degree in which students compile a portfolio of work, selecting at least three meaningful pieces that demonstrate key learning objectives and the themes of their course of study.
Final studio course in which students develop and critique a thesis project with novel designed artifacts and broader social and technical context.

Seminar accompanying the thesis process; prepares Master of Design students for the final semester and emphasizes design research and the role of the thesis in academic and professional settings, supporting thesis development, critique, and contextual framing.
Flexible elective slot used to complete the minimum unit requirement; students may choose approved courses aligned with their interests and program goals.

elective_requirements.requirement_description: One of the program’s elective units. Students select an approved course that complements the core curriculum and helps meet the 39-unit minimum.
elective_requirements.course_summary: Broad elective choice; the program materials show generic elective placeholders rather than a narrow subject restriction.

elective_requirements.requirement_description: One of the program’s elective units. Students select an approved course that complements the core curriculum and helps meet the 39-unit minimum.
elective_requirements.course_summary: Broad elective choice; the program materials show generic elective placeholders rather than a narrow subject restriction.

elective_requirements.requirement_description: One of the program’s elective units. Students select an approved course that complements the core curriculum and helps meet the 39-unit minimum.
elective_requirements.course_summary: Broad elective choice; the program materials show generic elective placeholders rather than a narrow subject restriction.

elective_requirements.requirement_description: One of the program’s elective units. Students select an approved course that complements the core curriculum and helps meet the 39-unit minimum.
elective_requirements.course_summary: Broad elective choice; the program materials show generic elective placeholders rather than a narrow subject restriction.

sources.llm_summary: States that over three semesters students complete eight required core courses and four electives to meet a minimum of 39 units.
Shows the year-by-year sequence including required courses, capstone portfolio, thesis studio, and elective placeholders, and notes a summer experience outside the classroom.
Describes the final studio course of the MDes program and its project-based thesis work.

sources.llm_title: Design Innovation Department Courses | UC Berkeley Catalog
sources.llm_summary: Identifies core required curriculum courses for the Master of Design program.

derivation_notes.note: Set to true because the program explicitly states that students complete required core courses.
Set to true because the program explicitly includes four electives.
Set to true because the sample plan shows generic elective placeholders and the program materials do not specify a narrow fixed list.
Set to true because the curriculum includes studio foundations, designing emerging technologies, and thesis studio in a prescribed sequence.
Set to true because the curriculum includes both a capstone portfolio and a thesis studio.
Set to true because the program expects a summer experience such as an internship, international design study, or community service outside the classroom.
Set to 39 based on the program’s stated minimum unit requirement.
Mapped to semester_credit_hours because the program uses Berkeley semester units.

Set to highly_sequenced: the official three-semester plan maps the required path term-by-term (fall, spring, second fall), so a high share of required items have an explicit designated term in program-design evidence—above the ~two-thirds threshold in sequencedness.json rules.

verification.verification_notes: Program claims are supported by Berkeley MDes and Berkeley Graduate Division pages, including the professional studio-based positioning, three-semester duration, minimum 39-unit tuition table, and required core/elective/thesis structure. Some curriculum-derived fields rely on program-plan interpretation and should be checked against the current official program design page if precision is needed.

identity.program_name: Master of Information Management and Systems
identity.credential_name: Master of Information Management and Systems

sources.llm_summary: Identifies the program as UC Berkeley's Master of Information Management and Systems and describes it as a two-year, full-time professional master's program.

sources.llm_title: UC Berkeley School of Information homepage
sources.llm_summary: Identifies the institution as the UC Berkeley School of Information and provides its Berkeley, California address.
Describes MIMS as educating information professionals to provide leadership for an information-driven world.
States that MIMS is a two-year full-time degree program requiring at least 48 semester units of study.

derived_features.positioning_summary: MIMS is positioned as a professional graduate program for information professionals, with explicit emphasis on interdisciplinary work bridging technology, people, and policy, and on preparing students for leadership in an information-driven world. The program also signals a strong user-research / UX-oriented dimension through its paths and alumni expertise, but the evidence does not directly support broader claims such as research-first or social-impact-first positioning.

sources.llm_summary: States that MIMS offers a uniquely interdisciplinary curriculum that bridges technology, people, and policy, and invites students to join the program.

Describes MIMS as educating information professionals to provide leadership for an information-driven world and notes student/alumni expertise in user experience design & research, product management, engineering, information policy, cybersecurity, and more.

Describes MIMS student work in user research, qualitative research methods, user interface design, information visualization, tangible interfaces, interface aesthetics, and inclusive principles for technology design.

Describes MIMS as a two-year full-time program designed to train students in the skills needed to succeed as information professionals and notes work across business, public sector, and academic settings.

derivation_notes.note: Added interdisciplinary because the page explicitly says the curriculum is 'uniquely interdisciplinary' and bridges technology, people, and policy.
Added professional because the page says the program educates 'information professionals' and prepares them to provide leadership for an information-driven world.
Added professional because the Graduate Division page says the program is designed to train students in the skills needed to succeed as information professionals.

Added stem_adjacent because the admissions page explicitly lists engineering among alumni expertise areas and the program sits in an information/computing context; this is directly supported by the page's own wording rather than general reputation.
Added ui_ux because the page explicitly mentions user research, user interface design, and interface aesthetics as student activities.
Added digital because the page explicitly discusses computer technology, user interfaces, information visualization, and tangible interfaces as part of the program's student work.
Did not add research: the page mentions user experience design & research, but the overall program positioning is not explicitly research-centered or scholarly-output oriented.
Did not add social_impact: the evidence mentions leadership and diverse backgrounds, but does not explicitly position the program around public interest, equity, sustainability, or civic impact.

sources.llm_summary: States that the MIMS program is a two-year, full-time professional master's program and that it requires at least 48 semester units of study.
Describes MIMS as a two-year full-time program.

sources.llm_title: MIMS Student Handbook for Students Entering Before Fall 2017
sources.llm_summary: States that students are required to complete the program within the two-year time frame and enroll in 12 units of graduate work each semester.

derivation_notes.note: Derived 4 Berkeley semesters from the page's explicit 'two-year' description, using Berkeley's standard two-semester academic year.
Assigned four_plus_semesters because two years corresponds to four standard semesters.

sources.llm_summary: Lists tuition and required fees for MIMS, including tuition, student services fee, Berkeley campus fee, transit fee, and UCGPC fee.

sources.llm_title: Tuition Costs & Fees - Berkeley Graduate Division
sources.llm_summary: Explains that graduate tuition and fees are estimates and that fees can include tuition, campus fee, transit fee, health insurance, nonresident fees, document management fees, and other program-specific fees.

derivation_notes.note: high — page presents tuition and fees in USD, so base currency is USD.
high — no conversion needed because the source amounts are already in USD; set exchange rate to 1.
high — no foreign exchange conversion was used, but the date reflects the extraction date for the USD-denominated figures.
Set to tuition_only because the evidence covers tuition and required fees, but not a full published cost of attendance total for the degree.
low — left null because the page provides component fees rather than a single explicit program-total figure comparable across programs.
low — left null because the funding page excerpt shows per-term fee components, but the excerpt does not provide a clearly stated full-degree total for MIMS.

derived_features.curriculum_summary: MIMS is a two-year, 48-semester-unit professional master's program with five required core courses, at least one advanced technology course, at least one advanced social science or policy course, electives for specialization, and an internship/final project component.

core_courses.course_summary: One of the five required core courses in the MIMS curriculum; the program page states that all students complete five core courses as part of the first-year core curriculum.
One of the five required core courses in the MIMS curriculum; the degree requirements page indicates a core-heavy first year with five core courses.
One of the five required core courses in the MIMS curriculum; the program page states that all students complete five core courses as part of the first-year core curriculum.
One of the five required core courses in the MIMS curriculum; the program requires five core courses in the first-year core curriculum.
One of the five required core courses in the MIMS curriculum; the program requires five core courses in total as part of the first-year core curriculum.

elective_requirements.requirement_description: All MIMS students must take at least one advanced technology course.
elective_requirements.course_summary: An elective or advanced course with a strong technology focus, such as computing, AI, engineering, or other technical topics.

elective_requirements.requirement_name: Advanced social science or policy course
elective_requirements.requirement_description: All MIMS students must take at least one advanced social science or policy course.
elective_requirements.course_summary: An elective or advanced course in social science, policy, ethics, law, or related contextual analysis areas.

elective_requirements.requirement_description: Students use electives to build advanced expertise in one or more information management specializations.
elective_requirements.course_summary: Any approved elective that supports a student's chosen specialization within information management and systems.

elective_requirements.requirement_description: Students may take additional electives from other UC Berkeley departments as part of the degree.
elective_requirements.course_summary: Electives taken outside the I School, subject to program approval and degree applicability.

sources.llm_summary: States that MIMS is a two-year, full-time professional master's program requiring at least 48 semester units and offering five core courses plus electives.
Describes the two-year full-time structure, five core courses, advanced technology and social science/policy requirements, electives, and specialization expectations.
Shows common focus areas and student work in UX, data science, policy, entrepreneurship, and related paths.

sources.llm_title: MIMS Student Handbook for Students Entering Before Fall 2017
sources.llm_summary: States that students are required to enroll in 12 units each semester and complete the program within two years.

derivation_notes.note: Set true because the program page explicitly says the MIMS program requires five core courses.
Set true because the degree requirements page explicitly describes electives and specialization through electives.
Set true because students may take additional electives from other UC Berkeley departments.
Set true because the program navigation and requirements context indicate a final project component, though the exact format is not fully detailed in the provided excerpts.
Set true because the program page explicitly includes internship in the requirements teaser.
Set to semester_credit_hours because the program states requirements in semester units.

Set to partially_sequenced: the first-year core is sequenced as a block, but in the modeled curriculum rows most required cores lack an explicit semester/term slot compared to a full term grid; share of term-designated required items stays below the highly_sequenced threshold (see sequencedness.json vs Berkeley MDes).

verification.verification_notes: Automated extraction only. Evidence supports the program identity, two-year/48-unit duration, tuition-only cost basis, and curriculum structure. Some curriculum details are partially inferred from page excerpts and should be human-checked.
